sentiment_score_definition: x <= -0.35: Bearish; -0.35 < x <= -0.15: Somewhat-Bearish; -0.15 < x < 0.15: Neutral; 0.15 <= x < 0.35: Somewhat_Bullish; x >= 0.35: Bullish
relevance_score_definition: 0 < x <= 1, with a higher score indicating higher relevance.

feed.title: Wipro expands Google Cloud partnership; rolls out Gemini Enterprise across global ops
feed.summary: Wipro has expanded its partnership with Google Cloud, adopting Gemini Enterprise across its global operations to enhance productivity and integrate AI-led workflows. This initiative, part of Wipro’s “Client Zero” strategy, aims to speed up decision-making and improve employee experience across various functions. The partnership also includes the launch of a new Google Cloud Gemini Experience Zone in Bengaluru to foster co-innovation for clients.

feed.title: Rocket Lab's $554M Revenue Dwarfs AST SpaceMobile's $18M Despite Identical $30B Valuations
feed.summary: Rocket Lab and AST SpaceMobile, despite both holding market caps near $30 billion, show vastly different financial performances. Rocket Lab boasts 30 times more revenue and stronger operational metrics, including gross margins of 37%, compared to AST SpaceMobile, which missed Q3 estimates and trades at a significantly higher price-to-sales ratio. This disparity highlights Rocket Lab as the clearer leader in execution and fundamental strength.

feed.title: LDOS: Citigroup Initiates Coverage with a 'Buy' Rating | LDOS St
feed.summary: Citigroup has initiated coverage on Leidos Holdings (LDOS) with a 'Buy' rating and set a price target of USD 218.00. This follows several other analysts who have maintained or raised their ratings and price targets for the IT services and solutions company, reflecting a positive outlook from the financial community. Wall Street analysts forecast an average target price of $219.18 for LDOS, implying a 16.55% upside from its current price.

feed.title: Abbott (ABT) Raises Quarterly Dividend by 6.8%
feed.summary: Abbott Laboratories (ABT) has increased its quarterly dividend by 6.8% to 63 cents per share, reflecting strong financial health with robust profitability and a solid balance sheet. The company, a significant player in healthcare with a $214.3 billion market cap, demonstrates attractive valuation metrics and positive analyst sentiment despite some insider selling. This dividend increase highlights Abbott's commitment to shareholder value and its stable financial position in the medical devices and instruments sector.

feed.title: Costco Delivers Another Strong Quarter — Analysts See Loyalty And Traffic Driving Growth
feed.summary: Costco Wholesale (NASDAQ: COST) reported strong fiscal first-quarter results, exceeding analyst expectations for earnings and total sales. Analysts from BofA Securities and Telsey Advisory Group maintained their positive ratings, highlighting that traffic, increased ticket size, member loyalty, and the company's business model are key drivers for continued growth. Costco's earnings per share came in at $4.50, surpassing consensus estimates, and total sales grew 8.3% to $67.3 billion.

feed.title: Danaher (NYSE:DHR) Stock Dips After Quarterly Earnings Update
feed.summary: Danaher (NYSE:DHR), a company providing global biotechnology, medical, and industrial solutions, saw its stock dip after a recent quarterly earnings update. The article focuses on Danaher's operational efficiency, technological integration, and corporate participation within the healthcare sector. It also briefly covers the company's main operational segments: Biotechnology, Industrial, and Commercial segments.

feed.title: The Best Turnaround Stock to Invest $1,000 in Right Now
feed.summary: This article examines Bristol Myers Squibb (BMY) and Pfizer (PFE) as potential turnaround investments, both facing patent cliffs that have impacted their stock performance. While Bristol Myers Squibb (BMY) offers a safer dividend play due to a more manageable payout ratio, Pfizer (PFE) presents a higher-risk, higher-reward opportunity with a larger stock price drop and more room for recovery, making it appealing for aggressive investors looking for significant upside.

feed.title: News | JPMorgan expands housing finance team; Transwestern adds research manager; EYA names multifamily chief
feed.summary: JPMorgan Chase appointed Karen Purcell to lead its community development banking team, focusing on affordable housing finance. Transwestern hired Jesse Tollison as a research manager for its Minneapolis office, and EYA named Robin Bettarel as head of multifamily development to expand its Washington D.C. pipeline. The article also details several other significant hires and promotions across various real estate firms.

feed.title: ResMed stock price target raised to $345 from $335 at UBS
feed.summary: UBS has increased its price target for ResMed (NYSE:RMD) to $345 from $335, reiterating a Buy rating. This adjustment reflects the firm's optimism due to the removal of Competitive Bidding concerns and an underestimation of potential impacts from GLP-1 weight loss medications on CPAP usage by the market. UBS projects significant growth in obstructive sleep apnea (OSA) users and a 13% annual EPS growth rate for ResMed through fiscal year 2029.

feed.title: Preferred Bank stock hits 52-week high at 99.63 USD By Investing.com
feed.summary: Preferred Bank (PFBC) stock reached a 52-week high of $99.63, reflecting a 7.2% appreciation over the past year and investor confidence due to strong financial performance. The bank's attractive value metrics include a P/E ratio of 9.94 and a 3.07% dividend yield, with management consistently buying back shares and maintaining dividends for 12 consecutive years. Despite an InvestingPro analysis suggesting it might be trading above its Fair Value, recent positive developments include strong Q3 2025 earnings and a 6.7% increase in the annual cash dividend.

feed.title: Procter & Gamble (PG) Stock News Today: Why Shares Are Near Lows, Analyst Forecasts, and What Comes Next (Dec. 12, 2025)
feed.summary: Procter & Gamble (NYSE: PG) stock is currently trading near its 52-week lows, primarily due to recent management commentary indicating volatile U.S. demand and a broad-based slowdown in consumer spending. Despite a "Moderate Buy" consensus from analysts, the market is re-evaluating the "defensive" nature of consumer staples amidst concerns about pricing power, volume erosion, and lingering tariff-related costs for 2025-2026. Investors are closely watching P&G's sustained FY2026 guidance and upcoming Q2 FY2026 earnings call for January 22, 2026, for signs of stabilization or further pressure on the stock.

feed.title: ImmunityBio, Moderna among winners of EU drug recommendations this week
feed.summary: The European Medicines Agency (EMA) has recommended seven new medicines for approval this week, including a cancer drug from ImmunityBio (IBRX) and Moderna's (MRNA) redesigned COVID shot. Other notable recommendations include Novo Nordisk's higher-dose Wegovy for obesity and expanded uses for several existing drugs. These endorsements could significantly impact the market positions and stock performance of the involved companies.

feed.title: Citigroup Initiates Coverage on HWM with a Buy Rating | HWM Stoc
feed.summary: Citigroup has initiated coverage on Howmet Aerospace (HWM) with a Buy rating and a price target of $246.00, adding to a recent series of positive analyst evaluations for the company. Despite positive analyst sentiment and an "Outperform" brokerage recommendation, GuruFocus estimates a potential 46.14% downside for HWM based on its GF Value.

feed.title: VoltaGrid, Halliburton secure 400MW of modular natural gas manufacturing capacity for AI data center market
feed.summary: VoltaGrid and Halliburton have secured manufacturing capacity for 400MW of modular natural gas power systems for data centers in the eastern hemisphere, with deployments expected by 2028. This partnership builds on their initial collaboration to provide distributed power generation for the global data center sector, focusing on the Middle East. The systems leverage VoltaGrid's QPac platform and Halliburton's logistics to deliver turnkey solutions for hyperscale data centers.

feed.title: Qualcomm Just Got Called an AI Loser—So Why Is It Rallying?
feed.summary: Despite a bearish outlook from Wedbush labeling Qualcomm an "AI loser" due to expected rising memory prices, Qualcomm's stock (QCOM) has rallied, gaining 12% in recent weeks. The company's strategic investments in edge AI, automotive, and IoT, combined with strong fundamentals and bullish technical indicators, contrast with Wedbush's focus on traditional PC and handset markets. Analysts from Susquehanna and Rosenblatt have reiterated their "Buy" ratings, suggesting Qualcomm is poised to defy the negative sentiment.

feed.title: Fiserv Stock News Today (Dec. 12, 2025): FISV Trades Near $69 as Security Lawsuit Headlines, Analyst Targets, and 2026 “Show-Me” Year Shape the Outlook
feed.summary: Fiserv (FISV) stock is trading around $69 amidst ongoing challenges, including a cybersecurity-related lawsuit from Self-Help Credit Union alleging weak security controls and persistent securities class-action reminders. The company is also still navigating the aftermath of its Q3 guidance reset and leadership changes, with analysts like JPMorgan labeling 2026 as a crucial "show-me" year for execution and investment. Investors are closely watching the "One Fiserv" turnaround plan, particularly the performance of its Clover growth engine, and upcoming catalysts like lawsuit developments and the next earnings window in early February 2026.

feed.title: The Walt Disney Company to invest more than 850 million euros in OpenAI: the deal will be for the next three years
feed.summary: The Walt Disney Company is set to invest over 850 million euros (approximately $1 billion) in OpenAI, solidifying a three-year content licensing deal. This partnership will enable Sora to generate short videos using characters and settings from Disney, Marvel, Pixar, and Star Wars, while ChatGPT Images will create AI-generated visuals. Both platforms are expected to begin producing content inspired by Disney characters and worlds in early 2026.

feed.title: Arcus Biosciences (RCUS) Shares Decline Over 11% in Recent Tradi
feed.summary: Arcus Biosciences (RCUS) shares fell by 11.2% in recent trading, indicating market volatility for the biotechnology company focused on cancer immunotherapies. The company faces significant financial challenges, with negative operating and net margins and declining revenue growth, alongside moderate balance sheet strength but an Altman Z-Score suggesting potential bankruptcy risk. Despite cautious optimism from analysts and a positive trend in moving averages, the stock carries risks including high volatility and insider selling.

feed.title: Analyst Updates: Nordson (NDSN) Price Target Raised by Baird | N
feed.summary: Baird has raised its price target for Nordson (NDSN) to $275 from $252, while maintaining a "Neutral" rating. This adjustment follows similar actions by other analysts who have also updated their price targets and ratings for the company. Nordson, a manufacturer of equipment for dispensing various materials, continues to receive varied assessments from analysts regarding its market performance and growth potential.

feed.title: Cisco, Amazon, and Siemens Set the Pace for Intelligent Hospitality Solutions Amid $90 Billion Market Opportunity
feed.summary: The smart hospitality market is expanding significantly, driven by digital transformation, sustainability, and automation. Key players like Cisco, Amazon, and Siemens are leading the integration of AI, cloud solutions, and robust technologies to enhance guest experiences and operational efficiency. The market is projected to grow from $35.7 billion in 2025 to $90.46 billion by 2032, presenting substantial opportunities for businesses adopting these intelligent solutions.

feed.title: Citigroup Initiates Coverage on General Dynamics (GD) with Neutral Rating | GD Stock News
feed.summary: Citigroup has initiated coverage on General Dynamics (GD) with a Neutral rating and an initial price target of $371 USD. This new coverage adds to a series of recent analyst ratings and adjustments for General Dynamics, reflecting diverse perspectives on the company's performance and future. The average target price from 19 analysts is $379.28, implying an 11.92% upside from its current price, while GuruFocus estimates a 1.45% downside based on its GF Value.

feed.title: Bank of America stock hits 52-week high at $54.83
feed.summary: Bank of America (BAC) stock reached a new 52-week high of $54.83, reflecting a 19.75% increase over the past year and a 27.02% year-to-date return. This surge highlights strong investor confidence in the bank, which maintains a 2.05% dividend yield and a 14.92 P/E ratio. Despite the rally, InvestingPro suggests the stock may still be undervalued based on its Fair Value assessment.

feed.title: UBS reiterates Neutral rating on Hubbell stock, sees 2026 growth below consensus
feed.summary: UBS maintained its Neutral rating and $450.00 price target on Hubbell (NYSE:HUBB), projecting 6% organic sales growth for 2026, which is below the current 7% consensus estimate. The investment firm anticipates "meaningful improvement in organic growth trends" in Q4 and into 2026, though it believes current market consensus estimates may require downward revision for 2026 EPS. Recent Q3 2025 earnings saw Hubbell exceed EPS expectations but miss on revenue, alongside pricing $400 million in senior notes to redeem existing debt.

feed.title: Textron stock hits 52-week high at 87.94 USD By Investing.com
feed.summary: Textron Inc. (TXT) stock recently achieved a 52-week high of $87.94, with the current trading price slightly higher at $88.12. The company has shown significant positive performance over the past year and year-to-date, reflecting strong investor confidence and a "GOOD" financial health score from InvestingPro. This milestone follows recent news including exceeding Q3 2025 adjusted EPS expectations, the type certification of its Cessna Citation Ascend business jet, and strategic management appointments.

feed.title: Abbott (NYSE: ABT) boosts dividend to $0.63, marking 408th straight quarterly payout
feed.summary: Abbott (NYSE: ABT) has announced a 6.8% increase in its quarterly common dividend, raising it to $0.63 per share. This marks the 54th consecutive year of dividend growth for the company and its 408th consecutive quarterly dividend payout since 1924. The dividend is payable on February 13, 2026, to shareholders of record as of January 15, 2026, reinforcing Abbott's position as an S&P 500 Dividend Aristocrat.

feed.title: American Tower (NYSE:AMT) Shares Gain After Strong Earnings Report
feed.summary: American Tower (NYSE:AMT) shares rose following a strong earnings report, indicating stable operations and robust revenue growth. The company, which operates as a real estate investment trust for communications infrastructure, noted significant institutional participation and strong operational efficiency across its global assets.

feed.title: IBM in Focus as Bernstein Maintains Neutral Stance After Confluent Acquisition
feed.summary: Bernstein SocGen Group has reiterated its Market Perform rating on IBM with a $280.00 price target, maintaining a neutral stance despite IBM's acquisition of Confluent. IBM will acquire Confluent for $31 per share in cash, an $11 billion enterprise value, with the transaction expected to close by mid-2026. While Bernstein sees strategic alignment, their overall view and valuation of IBM remain unchanged.

feed.title: TD Cowen Stays Bullish on Amazon (AMZN), Sees Multiple Growth Drivers Into 2026
feed.summary: TD Cowen reiterated a "Buy" rating on Amazon (AMZN) with a $300 price target, identifying it as their "Top Mega Cap Internet Pick." The firm anticipates strong growth drivers for Amazon into 2026, including accelerated AWS revenue, momentum in eCommerce and advertising, and continued operating margin expansion. These factors are expected to be fueled by AI workload demand, improved delivery speeds, and an expanding Prime Video ad business.

feed.title: Intuit (INTU) Stock News Today (Dec. 12, 2025): Insider Sales, Analyst Price Targets, and AI Catalysts Ahead of Earnings
feed.summary: Intuit (INTU) stock is stabilizing near the high-$600s as investors consider recent insider sales from directors Scott D. Cook and Richard L. Dalzell, alongside institutional position updates and a largely optimistic analyst consensus with price targets averaging around $800. The primary focus for the stock's future growth remains its "agentic AI" strategy, particularly how it will drive monetization and efficiency through products like QuickBooks and TurboTax ahead of the upcoming tax season and next earnings report in late February 2026.

feed.title: Nicholas Ray, CIO of Standard Motor, sells $345k in stock
feed.summary: Nicholas Ray, CIO & VP IT of Standard Motor Products, Inc. (NASDAQ:SMP), recently sold 9,093 shares of company stock for approximately $345,624. This transaction leaves him with 40,609 direct shares, in addition to ESOP shares. The company, with an $849 million market cap, maintains a 3.2% dividend yield and strong liquidity, though InvestingPro views it as slightly overvalued.

feed.title: Kroger adding $391 million distribution center in Simpson County
feed.summary: Kroger plans to establish a new $391 million distribution center in Simpson County, Kentucky, creating approximately 430 new full-time jobs. This facility will be a full-line distribution center with scalable and automated operations, supplementing a previous $204 million expansion by Kroger's Country Oven in Bowling Green. Governor Andy Beshear and Franklin Mayor Larry Dixon highlighted the significant economic impact and job creation this investment will bring to the state and local community.

feed.title: Salesforce (CRM) Stock News Today (Dec. 12, 2025): ValueAct’s $25M Insider Buy, FY2026 Forecast, and Agentforce Pricing Signals
feed.summary: Salesforce (CRM) is a key focus for investors due to several narratives: a significant insider purchase by ValueAct, an increased FY2026 outlook driven by AI "Agentforce" momentum, and evolving pricing strategies for its AI agents. The article details Salesforce's Q3 FY2026 results, its acquisition of Informatica, and analyst forecasts, highlighting the shift toward AI economics in its valuation. The company's future stock performance hinges on successfully monetizing AI and managing its ecosystem.

feed.title: Applied Materials (AMAT) Stock News Today: Dividend Update, Analyst Forecasts, and What to Watch Into 2026 (Dec. 12, 2025)
feed.summary: Applied Materials (AMAT) declared a quarterly dividend of $0.46 per share, maintaining a consistent shareholder return program. The company faces a balancing act between bullish analyst forecasts driven by AI-linked manufacturing and pressure points like export controls, China exposure, and valuation concerns. Investors are advised to watch earnings guidance, China policy signals, and overall AI capital expenditure sentiment.

feed.title: Terns Pharmaceuticals raises $747.5 million in public offering
feed.summary: Terns Pharmaceuticals (NASDAQ:TERN) successfully closed a public offering, raising $747.5 million in gross proceeds. The clinical-stage oncology company plans to use these funds for research, clinical trials, and potential commercial launch preparations for its lead product candidate, TERN-701, targeting chronic myeloid leukemia. This news follows a recent upsized offering and promising clinical data for TERN-701, leading to increased price targets from several analyst firms.

feed.title: Is RZLT Stock A Hidden Gem?
feed.summary: Rezolute Inc. (RZLT) stock has been volatile, recently rebounding to $1.295 after trading down significantly. Analysts see potential in the company's biopharmaceutical advancements and possible partnerships, despite recent financial setbacks. While the company shows strong liquidity, its price-to-book ratio suggests overvaluation, and management effectiveness needs improvement.

feed.title: Citigroup Maintains Neutral Rating, Raises Price Target for RES
feed.summary: Citigroup analyst Scott Gruber has maintained a "Neutral" rating for RPC (RES) and increased its price target from USD 5.75 to USD 6.00. This adjustment of 4.35% comes amid varying historical analyst ratings, with other firms like Freedom Capital Markets, Piper Sandler, Susquehanna, and Stifel providing different recommendations and price targets. RPC Inc. is an oilfield services company, and its stock's average target price from 6 analysts is $5.91, implying a slight upside from its current price.

feed.title: Telephone and Data Systems amends credit agreement, extends maturity and increases debt capacity
feed.summary: Telephone and Data Systems, Inc. announced a fourth amendment to its credit agreement, extending the maturity date to five years and removing credit spread adjustments. This amendment also increases the capacity for secured and unsecured debt by $300 million for TDS and its subsidiaries, reflecting the company's prudent financial management as indicated by a healthy current ratio and low debt-to-equity ratios. Separately, Array Digital Infrastructure, a TDS subsidiary, also amended its credit agreement.

feed.title: Direxion Daily Semiconductor Bull 3x Shares ETF Options Spot-On: On December 11th, 182.28K Contracts Were Traded, With 1.29 Million Open Interest
feed.summary: On December 11th, the Direxion Daily Semiconductor Bull 3x Shares ETF (SOXL.US) saw active options trading with 182.28K contracts traded, showing a near-even split between put and call options. The open interest for SOXL reached approximately 1.29 million contracts, representing 95.03% of its 30-day average. A notable trade was a put option with a strike price of $37.00 expiring on December 19th, 2025, which transacted 1,000 contracts for $43,000 turnover.

feed.title: article title here
feed.summary: brief 2-3 sentence summary of the article

feed.title: Arcus Biosciences (RCUS) Drops After Halting Phase 3 Cancer Tria
feed.summary: Arcus Biosciences (RCUS) shares fell by 14% after terminating a Phase 3 trial for its anti-TIGIT therapy, domvanalimab, due to a lack of improved overall survival in gastric and esophageal cancers. Despite this setback, the company maintains a strong financial position with $1 billion in cash and investments, allowing it to focus on other projects within its diverse pipeline of immunotherapies for cancer. The market capitalization stands at $2.67 billion, but the company faces challenges like negative operating margins and an Altman Z-Score indicating potential financial instability.

feed.title: Todd M. Schneider Joins PPG Board of Directors
feed.summary: PPG has announced that Todd M. Schneider, president and CEO of Cintas, will join its board of directors effective January 14, 2026. Schneider, who has over 35 years of experience at Cintas, will serve on the Nominating and Governance and Sustainability and Innovation committees. This appointment aims to strengthen PPG's board with Schneider's expertise in sales, service, and operations to support the company's long-term growth strategy.

feed.title: Mizuho Adjusts Price Target on EOG Resources to $134 From $133, Maintains Neutral Rating
feed.summary: Mizuho has increased its price target for EOG Resources (EOG) to $134 from $133, while reiterating a Neutral rating on the stock. This adjustment reflects a minor positive outlook on the company's valuation by Mizuho. The article also lists recent news and other analyst adjustments for EOG Resources.

feed.title: Duke Energy (NYSE: DUK) spotlights programs with $1 billion in customer bill savings
feed.summary: Duke Energy (NYSE: DUK) shared customer tips and highlighted programs to help reduce energy bills as frigid temperatures are expected next week. The company noted that participants in its efficiency programs have saved over $1 billion since 2019, encouraging customers to utilize resources like free home energy assessments and Smart $aver rebates. This outreach is part of Duke Energy's ongoing efforts to support customer relations and manage energy consumption alongside its broader energy transition strategy.

feed.title: Ecuador Ordered to Pay Amazon-Polluting Chevron $220 Million
feed.summary: An international arbitration panel has ordered Ecuador to pay Chevron $220 million, stemming from a decades-long legal battle over pollution perpetrated by Texaco (now Chevron) in the Ecuadorian Amazon. This decision compensates Chevron for legal fees despite the company’s documented ecological disaster, sparking outrage among affected Indigenous communities and critics of the investor-state dispute settlement (ISDS) system. The ruling highlights significant concerns about the power of international arbitration to override national sovereignty and undermine justice for environmental victims.

feed.title: Nvidia Corporation (NVDA) Bank of America Maintains Top Pick After Nvidia Meeting, Citing $500B AI Chip Demand Outlook
feed.summary: Bank of America has reiterated Nvidia as its "Top Pick" following an investor meeting, citing Nvidia's expectation of at least $500 billion in combined Blackwell, Rubin, and networking sales across 2025 and 2026. The company also projects its next-generation Blackwell architecture will be a full generation ahead of competitors. Despite some downside risks, analyst sentiment remains overwhelmingly positive with a "Strong Buy" rating and significant upside potential from current levels.

feed.title: POWER METALS REACHES CESIUM CONCENTRATE OFFTAKE AGREEMENT WITH ALBEMARLE
feed.summary: Power Metals Corp (TSXV: PWM) has announced an off-take agreement with Albemarle Corporation for cesium oxide concentrate. Albemarle will provide a CAD$5 million pre-payment for concentrate from Power Metals' Case Lake Project in Ontario, Canada. This agreement signifies a significant development for the Company's cesium operations.

feed.title: Collins Elbit Vision Systems' Zero-G Helmet Mounted Display System+ achieves key milestone with U.S. Navy
feed.summary: Collins Elbit Vision Systems (CEVS) has successfully completed the Critical Design Review for its Zero-G Helmet Mounted Display System+ (HMDS+) for the U.S. Navy's Improved Joint Helmet-Mounted Cueing System (IJHMCS) program. This sixth-generation helmet aims to enhance operational capabilities for F/A-18E/F Super Hornets and EA-18G Growlers by providing superior situational awareness and reducing aircrew strain. Initial operational capability is expected in 2027, with plans to field the system on over 750 U.S. Navy and Royal Australian Air Force aircraft.

feed.title: Billings Flying Service Acquired by Quanta Aviation Services
feed.summary: Billings Flying Service (BFS) has been acquired by Quanta Services, Inc. (NYSE: PWR), aiming to expand BFS's offerings into the utility and construction market and enhance Quanta Aviation Services' fleet with CH-47D aircraft. BFS will maintain its existing contracts and operational structure, while integrating into the Quanta Aviation Services family. This acquisition is expected to provide BFS with greater resources and long-term stability for growth.

feed.title: Sell Alert: Kristian Talvitie Cashes Out $8.71M In PTC Stock
feed.summary: Kristian Talvitie, EVP at PTC, recently sold 50,291 shares of PTC stock for a total of $8,707,634. This insider transaction, filed with the SEC, occurred on December 11, causing PTC shares to be down slightly. The article details PTC's financial performance, including significant revenue growth and strong profitability metrics, and explains the importance of insider activity in financial analysis.

feed.title: Spotlight on KKR: Analyzing the Surge in Options Activity
feed.summary: High-rolling investors have shown bullish sentiment towards KKR (NYSE: KKR) through significant options activity, with 9 options trades detected, predominantly calls. Traders are targeting a price window of $125.0 to $145.0, and a recent analyst rating from UBS set a $176 price target for the stock, despite a slight downward revision to a "Buy" rating. KKR, a major alternative asset manager, is currently trading at $142.55 with its next earnings report 53 days away.
